topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed with a notification that made my stomach clench. A profile picture popped up: a grinning face I hadn't seen in fifteen years, a face that used to bring a sharp, unpleasant tightening to my chest. It was Mark. The name alone sent a wave of coldness washing over me. My fingers hovered over the screen, the glass cool beneath my touch. What did I do? A small tremor ran through my hand as I considered the options: accept, ignore, block. The silence in my room suddenly felt thick, suffocating. I stared at the little blue circle indicating the pending request, my breath catching in my throat. The urge to slam the device down was intense, but a strange hesitation kept my thumb paralyzed above the button.

[story 2]

The chime sounded like an alarm in the quiet evening. I glanced at the screen, and my heart began to pound a frantic rhythm against my ribs. It was him. The familiar, sneering expression stared back at me from the photo. A flush crept up my neck, warm and prickly. Every instinct screamed at me to delete the notification, to hide the phone under my pillow. I could feel my palms growing slick with sweat. I took a deep, shaky breath, the air tasting thin in my lungs. Then, slowly, deliberately, I tapped the button. The ensuing silence was deafening, punctuated only by the frantic drumming inside my own chest.

[story 3]

When the notification appeared, I froze mid-scroll. It was an unwelcome intrusion, a ghost from a past I desperately tried to bury. The image of him—that smirk etched onto his face—made my skin crawl. A knot formed tight in my chest, squeezing the air out of me. I felt a sudden, overwhelming need to retreat, to vanish into the shadows of my room. My gaze darted around the room as if searching for an escape route. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the strain in my neck muscles. Slowly, my hand moved, trembling slightly, and I tapped the acceptance icon. The weight of the decision settled heavily upon my shoulders.
